validation_gaps: No preflight sentinel guards the 5 TkRecordFailureFromErr injection sites in upstream-shaped files; a future upstream merge that modifies error paths could silently drop passive availability taps without any automated failure.
GeminiV1BetaListModels upstream-proxy path (writeUpstreamResponse) is unfiltered — noted in design doc §8 遗留事项 but no test pin exists to detect if the fallback paths ever gain the proxy's pattern.

findings.description: The 5 passive availability failure tap calls (TkRecordFailureFromErr at gateway_handler.go:468, :829; chat_completions:247; responses:253; gemini_v1beta:497) are 1-line injections in upstream-shaped files with no machine-enforceable guard. The newapi sentinel registry protects newapi fifth-platform surfaces; there is no equivalent for the pricing-availability tap hooks. A future upstream merge that modifies the error-handling sections in these 4 files (even via conflict resolution) could silently drop all or some taps without any preflight failure, breaking the passive observability foundation silently. Per CLAUDE.md §「升级原则」: when a load-bearing TK injection in an upstream file could be lost on merge, it MUST be guarded by a preflight check, not by convention alone.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a new sentinel registry `scripts/pricing-availability-sentinels.json` listing the 5 tap call sites as must_contain entries, following the exact same structure as newapi-sentinels.json. Add a check in `scripts/preflight.sh` (after the existing §10 newapi sentinel block) that runs `python3 scripts/check-pricing-availability-sentinels.py --quiet` (reuse the same script with a different registry path, or add a --registry flag). This converts the human-convention rule into a CI gate. The check should verify: (a) gateway_handler.go contains TkRecordFailureFromErr at least twice, (b) gateway_handler_chat_completions.go contains TkRecordFailureFromErr, (c) gateway_handler_responses.go contains TkRecordFailureFromErr, (d) gemini_v1beta_handler.go contains TkRecordFailureFromErr, (e) gateway_service.go contains tkPricingAvailability.RecordOutcome.

findings.description: ModelListFilter.PricedCandidates() is an exported method with zero callers after the AntigravityModels fix (commit c2b271b8 replaced PricedCandidates with tkAntigravityDefaultModels). Keeping an exported function with no caller creates a misleading extension surface and conflicts with CLAUDE.md principle of no backwards-compatibility shims. The method comment says 'suitable as the initial candidate set for platforms that don't derive candidates from account model_mappings' but no such platform currently uses it.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete PricedCandidates() from model_list_filter_tk.go. If a future platform genuinely needs an all-catalog candidate set, the method can be re-added at that time with a real caller. Also update gateway_handler_tk_model_list_test.go line 22 which references it in a comment.
